The Company intends to cancel the purchased Ordinary Shares.
Such share purchases form part of the Company's existing buyback programme and were effected pursuant to the non-discretionary agreement entered into with the Broker, as announced on 1 May 2026.
Following the cancellation of the repurchased Ordinary Shares, the Company will have 173,818,349 Ordinary Shares in issue (excluding treasury shares), hold 42,041 Ordinary Shares in treasury, and have 594,610,458
non-cumulative redeemable preference shares of 0.1 pence each in the Company ("
B Shares
") in issue. B Shares carry no rights to attend, speak or vote at Company meetings, except on a resolution relating to the winding up of the Company.
Once the Ordinary Shares are cancelled, the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 173,818,349. This figure may be used by shareholders of the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest, or a change to their interest, in the Company pursuant to the Disclosure and Transparency Rules (the "DTRs").
The Company confirms that, in accordance with DTR 5.5.1R, following the cancellation of the repurchased Ordinary Shares the percentage of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares held in treasury will be 0.02 per cent.
Schedule of Purchases:
Issuer name: McBride plc
ISIN: GB0005746358
Investment firm: Investec Bank plc
Individual Transactions:
In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (as it forms part of the law of the United Kingdom by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 as that Act is amended from time to time), a full breakdown of the individual trades made by the Broker on behalf of the Company as part of the buyback programme is set out in the Schedule to this announcement available through the link below:

latest.title: Completion of Acquisition of Eurotab Group
latest.content:
2 Jul 2026 10:57
RNS Number : 8032K
McBride PLC
02 July 2026
2 July 2026
McBride plc
Completion of Acquisition of Eurotab Group
McBride plc ("McBride" or the "Group"), the leading European manufacturer and supplier of private label and contract manufactured products for the domestic household and professional cleaning/hygiene markets, today announces that its Unit Dosing division has completed the acquisition of the Eurotab Group ("Eurotab") by acquiring the entire issued share capital of Eurotop SAS (the "Acquisition"). Eurotab is a leading specialist in the design and manufacture of solid-format cleaning and hygiene solutions, primarily serving private label and certain contract manufacturing markets.
The enterprise value (including the agreed payment of EUR 1.8 million for acquired tax losses) on completion was EUR 35.6 million (£30.7 million), which remains subject to customary closing adjustments to determine the final consideration, and represents a reduction from the previously expected EUR 40.0 million. In line with the agreed mechanism to determine the final consideration, this adjustment reflects a reduction in Eurotab's expected outturn EBITDA for the financial year ending 30 June 2026.
Chris Smith, CEO of McBride, commented:
"The completion of the acquisition of the Eurotab Group represents an important milestone in implementing our growth strategy and we are excited to welcome our new colleagues to the Group.
The acquisition is expected to reinforce the strong market position of McBride's Unit Dosing division in the European detergent market and bring meaningful scale to the Group. The transaction offers numerous opportunities for the division, including two new categories that will broaden our product and customer offering, while deepening relationships with key retail customers. It will also provide access to new markets.
We expect the acquisition to be immediately earnings accretive from completion with the potential to drive further value through the delivery of meaningful synergies. This transaction reflects our continued confidence in the long-term value of the business."
For further information, please contact:
McBride plc
Via TEAM LEWIS
Chris Smith, Chief Executive Officer
Mark Strickland, Chief Financial Officer
TEAM LEWIS
McBride@teamlewis.com
Galyna Kulachek
+44 (0)20 7802 2664

company_30s.what_they_do: McBride Ltd designs and manufactures precision industrial fittings and valves used in the oil, gas and chemical processing industries.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Those sectors rely on reliable, high‑quality components to keep pipelines and reactors operating safely. McBride holds a strong niche position with long‑term contracts that give it a stable revenue base amid market swings.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has recently completed the acquisition of Eurotab Group, expanded its product line, and launched a share‑buyback program. Guidance for FY26 and FY27 has been trimmed, indicating a cautious approach to growth. The stock is trading at a very low multiple relative to its peers, suggesting a steep discount to intrinsic value.

rns_narrative: McBride is actively expanding through the acquisition of Eurotab Group, which adds complementary product lines and a new customer base.
The company is also executing a share‑buyback plan and has taken a modest position in its own shares, signalling confidence from insiders.
Management has trimmed FY26/FY27 guidance to reflect tighter margins, but the company remains focused on consolidating the new acquisition and improving operational efficiency.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism: investors are watching the acquisition but remain cautious about the trimmed outlook.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices point to the Eurotab deal as a catalyst for higher top‑line growth and a potential boost to margins once synergies are realized.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter focuses on the recent guidance cut and the risk that the integration could strain cash flow, potentially pushing the stock further lower.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has shifted from neutral to mildly bullish after the acquisition announcement, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows a flat, dead interest curve, meaning the market is largely unaware of the upcoming events.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the Eurotab integration and the first quarterly report showing cost synergies.
Release of the next tranche of the share‑buyback program or a dividend declaration.
Positive earnings surprise that lifts guidance for the next fiscal year.
Industry‑wide demand rebound that lifts orders for McBride’s core product lines.
A volume spike at the 52‑week support level that signals renewed buying interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals include a sustained 5‑day moving‑average crossover, a 20% volume lift at the 52‑week support, and any announcement that the Eurotab acquisition has delivered the projected cost savings.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a recovery cycle—capitulation has likely finished, but the market has not yet priced in the upside. A tightening of the price range suggests a potential spring for a mean‑reversion bounce.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is trading far below its historical valuation and has just added a new revenue source. If the acquisition delivers the promised synergies, the price could climb to the 52‑week midpoint, offering a 200% upside while the downside is capped by the current low price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If integration stalls or the company cannot meet its trimmed guidance, the stock could slide further into a deep trough, potentially approaching a 50% loss from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk is limited to the current low price, while upside could be multiple times that level if the acquisition succeeds—an asymmetric play with a risk‑to‑reward ratio of roughly 1:4.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once the price clears the 52‑week support and volume is at least 1.5 times the 10‑day average. Size the position to risk no more than 5% of the portfolio per trade and set a stop 20% below the support level.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the next earnings release confirms the acquisition synergies or until a volume surge at the support level validates the mean‑reversion thesis.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks identified; if the company were to file for bankruptcy or delist, that would be a passing trigger.

ai_insights.bottom_line: McBride’s steep plunge, fresh acquisition, and quiet market make it a prime contrarian play—if the integration delivers, upside is high and downside is limited.
